data.title: C3.ai whipsaws as analysts stay positive despite 'mixed guidance'
data.description:
Shares of enterprise software maker C3.ai (NYSE:AI) whipsawed on Tuesday — oscillating between gains and losses — as analysts stayed positive on the Thomas Siebel-led company after it reported better-than-expected quarterly results and upped its outlook.

JMP Securities maintained Market Outperform rating on C3.ai and increased its price target to $55 from $40 after the company announced better-than-expected second quarter fiscal 2025 results with accelerating growth.

Analysts led by Patrick Walravens said the company reported mixed guidance for fiscal third quarter and the year with — the third quarter non-GAAP operating loss range of -$38.6M to -$46.6M, consensus of -$30.5M on revenue of $95.5M to $100.5M, midpoint at $98M (consensus $97.6M); full-year operating loss of -$105M to -$135M, consensus -$108M on revenue of $378M to $398M, midpoint at $388M (consensus $383M), representing growth of around 25% at the midpoint.

The company expects to be free cash flow negative in the fiscal third quarter, consensus -$15M; free cash flow positive in fiscal fourth quarter 2025, implying negative free cash flow in fiscal 2025, but commentary expecting positive free cashflow in fiscal 2026, according to the analysts.

The analysts added that while C3.ai continues to face a number of risks, including its dependency on Baker Hughes, which has represented about 20% of fiscal 2025 revenue and whose current contract expires in June 2025, overall, they continue to like the story for several reasons, including that — the company has now had seven quarters of accelerating revenue growth; and C3.ai offers a range of AI applications for enterprises in fields like manufacturing, defense, government, and oil and gas, and has deep AI domain expertise.

Walravens and his team also like C3.ai because the company announced a broad new six-year partnership with Microsoft that establishes "C3.ai as a preferred AI application partner on Azure" and allows Microsoft customers to buy C3.ai applications through the Azure Portal and "on Microsoft Paper," which should make contracting easier for the company.

In addition, the analysts said that C3.ai is reducing its revenue dependency on Baker Hughes, which was 35% of the company's revenue in fiscal 2023, 27% in fiscal 24, 22% in fiscal first quarter 2025 and 18% in fiscal second quarter. The company is also expecting to see increased demand in areas like federal and defense following the new government administration with C3 AI's Chairman and CEO Thomas Siebel noting that there has been a shift in interest from "submarines and carriers and space, and aircraft" to the "AI applications, AI applications, AI applications, AI applications, and then cyber."

Siebel has an in-depth experience in the software industry and navigated his prior company, Siebel Systems, to a good outcome in its sale to Oracle in 2006 for $5.8B, the analysts added.

Piper Sandler maintained its Neutral rating and raised the price target to $42 from $24.

Analyst Arvind Ramnani and his team said that C3.ai posted impressive fiscal third quarter results, with revenues of $94.3M (+8.2% quarter-over-quarter) ahead of Street's $91M (+4.3%). Growth was driven by continued momentum both across Federal and commercial segments, including new logos and growing client wallet share.

The analysts noted that C3.ai's expanded agreement with Microsoft is still in its early stages and, they think, could potentially drive material upside (which is not incorporated in their/consensus estimates).

C3.ai management emphasized that it remains confident in expanding its Federal business with the new administration (as AI spend remains a top priority). In addition, the company outlined that it is exploring several options with Baker Hughes as it comes up for renewal (balancing the stability of a large client and reliability with having the flexibility for new growth vectors), according to the analysts.

Ramnani and his team noted that from a margin perspective, the company is prioritizing growth over profitability given the vast opportunity that it sees for enterprise AI.

Related stocks: Snowflake (SNOW) -1%, Palantir (PLTR), -1%, SoundHound AI (SOUN) -4%, BigBear.ai (BBAI) -6%, while Intapp (INTA) and SAP (SAP) were largely flat premarket on Tuesday.

data.title: BlackRock CFO: ETFs are a 'lubricating agent for business-model transformation'
data.description:
The exchange-traded fund industry has been expanding at a robust clip in recent years -- with room for further growth -- "because it's a lubricating agent for business-model transformation," Martin S. Small, chief financial officer of BlackRock (NYSE:BLK), said on Tuesday in a fireside chat at an industry event.

BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) is the world's largest asset manager and the biggest ETF provider. As of October, the owner of iShares had the largest market share of ETFs in the U.S., at nearly 31%.

Small explained that ETFs make it easier for financial firms to adapt and grow by offering new ways to invest, reach more clients and stay ahead of market trends. He pointed to two growth areas within BlackRock's (NYSE:BLK) ETF business: Europe's market share opportunity and fixed income ETFs.

With a suite of more than 1,500 ETFs, the company has been addressing investor demand by offering a diverse range of products tailored to various asset classes and investment strategies, from bitcoin to artificial intelligence to dividend-paying international stocks.

data.title: Solaris Energy Infrastructure announces pricing of underwritten public offering of class A common stock
data.description:
• Solaris Energy Infrastructure (NYSE:SEI) on Tuesday announced the pricing of its underwritten public offering of 6.5M shares of Class A common stock at a public offering price of $24.75 per share, for total net proceeds of about $156 million.
  • In connection with the offering, Yorktown Energy Partners X, a selling stockholder of the company, has granted the underwriters the option to purchase up to an additional 975,000 shares of Class A common stock on the same terms and conditions within 30 days.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 11.
  • The company intends to contribute all of the net proceeds it receives from the offering to Solaris Energy Infrastructure in exchange for a number of Solaris LLC membership units equal to the number of shares of Class A common stock issued in the offering.
  • Solaris will use the net proceeds to fund growth capital for additional power generation equipment, including new natural gas turbines and complementary “balance of plant” electrical equipment, to support customer activity.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of the Option Shares offered by Yorktown.
  • SEI is +14.33% to $29.44.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ohio Supreme Court rules in favor of pharmacy chains in opioid case
data.description:
The Ohio Supreme Court has reportedly ruled in favor of CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), Walgreens (NASDAQ:WBA) and Walmart (NYSE:WMT) in a case that involved two counties suing the pharmacy chains for creating a public nuisance with their dispensing of opioid medications.

The counties, Lake and Trumbull, were awarded $651M by a lower court in August. On Tuesday, the Ohio Supreme Court found that a state product liability law prevented the counties from suing on public nuisance grounds, according to Reuters.

The ruling could result in the award being overturned. Lake and Trumbull counties had opted out of nationwide settlements reached with makers and distributors of opioid medications, opting instead to pursue their own cases, Reuters added.

data.title: Activist investor Ancora pitches Amcor with its planned acquisition of Berry
data.description:
Activist investor Amcor (NYSE:AMCR) pitched Amcor at an investor conference on the merits of its planned $8.4 billion all-stock takeover of Berry Global (BERY) that will combine the two packaging companies.

Ancora believes Amcor (AMCR) may have 60% upside to $16 a share from its takeover of Berry (BERY), Ancora President Jim Chadwick said at the Bloomberg Activism Forum in New York City on Tuesday.

Chadwick sees limited to zero U.S. antitrust issues with a potential deal, as the businesses are highly complimentary.

Packaging solutions company Amcor (AMCR) agreed to acquire peer Berry (BERY) in an $8.4B all-stock transaction last month.

data.title: Barrick Gold said to seek buyer for Ivory Coast mine- report
data.description:
Barrick Gold (NYSE:GOLD) is looking for buyers for its Tongon gold mine in the Ivory Coast, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The gold miner started working with Toronto-Dominion Bank to identify buyers and solicit bids for the mine in late November, according to the report.

Barrick (GOLD) plans to stop production at the 24-year-old Tongon mine by 2027. The mine produced 204,000 ounces last year, which was about 5% of the company’s overall gold output for the year.

Barrick has about 90% stake in Tongon, with the Ivory Coast government and local investors holding the rest of the operation.

Barrick did not immediately respond to a Seeking Alpha request for comment on the report.

Barrick Gold (GOLD) shares were up more than 1% in afternoon trading.

data.title: U.S. small business optimism jumps to highest level in over three years on Trump boost
data.description:
A key monthly measure of optimism among U.S. small business owners on Tuesday surged to its highest level since June 2021, primarily driven by Donald Trump's election victory.

The NFIB small business optimism index rose by eight points in November to 101.7, after 34 months of remaining below the 50-year average of 98.

Small businesses form a big bulk of the U.S. economy. The Small Business Administration (SBA) is responsible for helping entrepreneurs and small businesses get the resources needed to start or expand their profession or to recover from disasters.

According to the SBA, the agency in FY 2025 will support at least $43B in 7(a), 504 and micro small business loans, over $7B in bonding (including both bids and final bonds), and over $45B in overall investment capitalization to startups and growth-oriented firms.

“The election results signal a major shift in economic policy, leading to a surge in optimism among small business owners,” NFIB chief economist Bill Dunkelberg said in a statement on Tuesday.

“Main Street also became more certain about future business conditions following the election, breaking a nearly three-year streak of record high uncertainty. Owners are particularly hopeful for tax and regulation policies that favor strong economic growth as well as relief from inflationary pressures. In addition, small business owners are eager to expand their operations,” Dunkelberg added.

The state of the economy was found to be the top concern among voters in the U.S. presidential election. Since Trump's victory, Wall Street has scaled fresh record highs amid an expectation that the former President's second term will once again pursue tax-cut, tariff-based policies and will attempt to stimulate economic growth.

In Tuesday's data from the NFIB, the net percent of small business owners expecting the economy to improve rose 41 points in November from October to a net 36%, the highest since June 2020. This component had the greatest impact on the overall increase in the optimism index.

Additionally, the NFIB found that twenty percent of owners in November indicated inflation as their single most important problem in operating their business (higher input and labor costs), down three points from October and surpassing labor quality as the top issue by one point.

The NFIB has collected Small Business economic trends data with quarterly surveys since the fourth quarter of 1973 and monthly surveys since 1986.

For investors looking to track the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) index, here are some exchange-traded funds of interest: (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

For investors looking to follow the federal government through market instruments, here are some politically driven Republican and Democratic ETFs:

  • God Bless America ETF (YALL)
  • American Conservative Values ETF (ACVF)
  • Point Bridge America First ETF (MAGA)
  • Democratic Large Cap Core ETF (DEMZ)
  • Unusual Whales Subversive Democratic ETF (NANC)
  • Unusual Whales Subversive Republican ETF (KRUZ)

data.title: Capital One sees closing Discover deal early 2025, adding 'rare and valuable' asset
data.description:
Discover Financial Service's (DFS) card network is a "rare and valuable asset," Capital One Financial (NYSE:COF) Chairman and CEO Richard Fairbank said Tuesday at an industry conference.

It's a piece that connects the credit card issuer with merchants, he noted. The acquisition is a "unique opportunity" for Capital One (COF) to bring the scale that the Discover network needs.

Capital One (COF) remains enthusiastic about the pending acquisition of Discover (DFS). "Everything that we can see, we continue to be very excited," Fairbank said. "What we expected going in is still pretty consistent with what we're seeing."

Fairbank is expecting the Discover (DFS) shareholder vote on the deal will occur early next in 2025. Capital One (COF) has submitted everything needed by the banking regulators. The process with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency and the Federal Reserve, its two banking regulators, continues to move along. The company is also continuing to engage with the Federal Trade Commission.

SEC has asked Discover to file a restatement of financials relative to issues on the network pricing side. While that's creating a delay, it hasn't affected the earnings.

The deal's economics are still within the range Capital One expected when it agreed to the acquisition, said Jeff Norris, senior vice president of finance at the company.

The company expects to be in a strong excess capital after the acquisition closes, he noted. It's likely to lead to increased distributions to shareholders, Norris said, without being specific.

Capital One (COF) stock edged down 0.3% in afternoon trading.

Looking toward the broader economy, "the consumer is a source of strength in the economy these days, and has been for quite some time," Fairbank said. Wages are finally increasing. The savings rate is still pretty healthy, assisted by a recent revision in the saving number.

"I think the consumer is in quite a good place," he said.

Charge-offs are a bit higher for Capital One (COF) and for the industry than pre-pandemic. "Credit is very much settling out," from the unusually low levels during the pandemic.

The reason for that is a "mathematical" phenomenon, as recoveries of charged-off debt are running much lower than prepandemic levels. Inventory amounts have been lower, leading to lower recoveries, he explained.

He also cited a delayed charge-off effect of the pandemic. During the crisis, there was unprecedented government stimulus, resulting in a massive surplus that came to consumers. That may have tided over consumers for a while, but their underlying situation didn't improve.

"Credit losses play out by things that are happening at the margins."

Capital One (COF) is "running at notably higher payment rates than prepandemic, but percentage of people paying a lower portion of their balance is also increasing," Fairbank said. "That may explain why elevated charge-offs are playing out."

Capital One's (COF) purchase volume vs. prepandemic is about 45% higher. However, much of that is the company's growth, he observed. Spend per consumer is basically flat. Weakness in purchase volume was coming in at the lower end of the market.

Overall, "things are pretty stable" for the average consumer on the purchase volume side, he said.
